justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 test present using Promise.allSettled with mock returning false. AC4 tested. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence section does NOT acknowledge that AC3 requires integration/concurrency testing to fully verify — low_confidence_areas is empty and there is no mention that the unit test only simulates the race via mocks rather than exercising true concurrent execution.

observations.text: Correct optimistic locking pattern: updateSeatStatus called with expectedCurrentStatus='available'
AC3 race condition tested with Promise.allSettled — mock updateSeatStatus returning false after successful findSeat
Fast-path optimization for AC2 (skip write if already reserved) is a good design choice
All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage with clear notes
ReservationError with typed code field is clean error handling
Missing acknowledgment that AC3 unit test only simulates the race via mocks — true concurrency testing requires integration tests or stress tests against a real database
low_confidence_areas is empty despite AC3 concurrency being inherently hard to fully verify with unit tests alone — 98% confidence is overconfident on this point
